Issue-linkage assertions are the primary differentiator: with-skill passes both issue-link checks while without-skill fails those checks in eval 0 and eval 1.
All non-linkage assertions pass in both configurations (single commit, push success, PR URL present), so these checks validate baseline workflow but are weak discriminators.
No timing regression appears in this run set (0.2s mean in both configs), suggesting skill quality gains are not coming with measurable latency in this fixture.
Token usage is modestly higher with-skill (+44 mean), consistent with extra commit-body/PR-body structure rather than extra workflow steps.

Eval 2 (no linked issue) is non-discriminating in current form because both configs pass every assertion; consider adding an assertion about concise summary formatting quality if you want stronger separation there.
